10.display: (0, 0) — this means a and b are identical vectors (equal in both size and direction)

13.display: Correct — vectors are added component by component: adding the top (horizontal) values together and the bottom (vertical) values together separately.

14.display: True — multiplying a vector by a positive scalar keeps the same direction and scales the length by that factor.

15.display: Correct — e.g. if $\mathbf{a} = \binom{3}{2}$, then $-\mathbf{a} = \binom{-3}{-2}$, which has the same length but points in exactly the opposite direction.

16.display: Always — a vector added to its negative always cancels out to the zero vector.

17.display: True — a scalar multiple only stretches, shrinks, or reverses a vector along the same line, so it always points in the same direction (or exactly opposite), which is the definition of parallel vectors.

18.display: $\mathbf{a} - \mathbf{b} = \binom{3}{-2}$ and $\mathbf{b} - \mathbf{a} = \binom{-3}{2}$ — they are negatives of each other, with the same length but opposite direction.

19.display: Wrong — scalar multiplication multiplies every component, so $3\mathbf{a} = \binom{6}{15}$, not adding 3 to each part.

20.display: $\overrightarrow{AB} = \overrightarrow{OB} - \overrightarrow{OA} = \mathbf{b} - \mathbf{a}$, since travelling from A to B is the same as going back to O then forward to B.

23.display: $\overrightarrow{OB} = \mathbf{a} + \mathbf{c}$, since $AB$ is equal and parallel to $OC$ in the parallelogram, so travelling O→A→B is the same as adding $\mathbf{a}$ and $\mathbf{c}$.

26.display: $\overrightarrow{AC} = (3, 3)$ — because going from A to B then B to C ends at the same place as going directly from A to C, so the vectors add.

29.display: $\overrightarrow{OM} = \frac{1}{2}(\mathbf{a}+\mathbf{c})$, which is also the midpoint of $OB$ (since $\overrightarrow{OB} = \mathbf{a}+\mathbf{c}$), showing both diagonals share the same midpoint M, so they bisect each other.

30.display: Your own labelled triangle or parallelogram diagram with vectors a and b, and the correctly worked-out unknown vector.
